response: The internet began in the 1960s with US Department of Defense-funded research, leading to ARPANET's first message in 1969. The transition to TCP/IP in 1983 laid the groundwork for the modern internet, while Tim Berners-Lee's invention of the World Wide Web in 1989 made it more accessible. The dot-com boom of the late 1990s, followed by the 2001 crash, saw companies like Amazon and Google rise to prominence. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and is integral to nearly all aspects of modern life.
